Off Calendar Oct-27-2025 Continued To Apr-27-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
K. Greg Peterson, for and with John Jennings, Administrator.

At the court's query, Mr. Peterson reports that he will be representing Administrator moving forward and intends to file a substitution of attorney shortly. Mr. Peterson reports as to the status of this matter and of a related probate matter in San Mateo County.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Apr-27-2026 at 9:00 AM in Dept. 204.

Off Calendar Apr-07-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-07-2025 Continued To Jun-16-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Jennings, Administrator (via Zoom).

Mr. Jennings stated he has engaged a CPA to assist with the final inventory and appraisal, but efforts have stalled due to unclear instructions from the document preparer and additional fees being requested. He expressed frustration over the lack of progress and support and described personal and procedural challenges that have delayed the administration of the estate.

The court reminded Mr. Jennings that the case has been pending for over five years, and that failure to appear at scheduled hearings wastes court resources. The Court acknowledged Mr. Jennings' personal challenges and took the Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause off calendar.

The court continued the matter to June 16, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Mr. Jennings was directed to file a petition for final distribution prior to that date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-28-2024 Continued To Dec-30-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
John Jennings, in propria persona, Administrator.

At the court's query, Mr. Jennings reports as to why he failed to appear at the three (3) previous callings. The court is satisfied with Mr. Jennings' report and the Orders to Appear are taken off calendar. Mr. Jennings offers a report as to the status of the Estate. The court and Mr. Jennings discuss and agree to a continuance date. The court reminds Mr. Jennings that he needs to file a petition and a Final Inventory and Appraisal prior to the next hearing date.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is continued to Dec-30-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Belen. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-16-2024 Continued To Oct-28-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The court noted that Mr. Jennings has not come to court for a couple of hearings and expressed concern about the administrator's health as Mr. Jennings had indicated at his last appearance on May 8th that he had not been feeling well. Status on close of estate and the order to appear are continued to October 28, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A further Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator John Jennings to appear on October 28, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he failed to appear and why a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration has not been filed. The court stated that it is considering issuing an order to show cause regarding suspension and removal of the administrator and that it may have to get the Public Administrator involved.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: E. Melendez. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel - Civil
Off Calendar Mar-17-2023 Granted Per Written Order

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Mar-15-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Randolph Stein appeared by zoom for and with John Jennings (administrator) by zoom.

Counsel and Mr. Jennings spoke of the breakdown in their relationship, the lack of their communication and the reasons why the required documents have not been filed and why it is appropriate for Mr. Stein's withdrawal. Mr. Jennings stated that there is $186,000.00 in the estate account, that there is also a Fidelity account and a Wells Fargo account, and that the amount of funds will be more. Mr. Jennings also stated that he will be working with the People's Choice and will be representing himself. The First Status Report is off calendar. The Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel is under submission with the intent to grant. Status on filing of an inventory and appraisal is set for June 7, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., n Dept. 204. The court orders Mr. Jennings not to make any disbursements without a court order.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel - Civil
Off Calendar Feb-06-2023 Continued To Mar-15-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-30-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-19-2022 Continued To Nov-30-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Randolph Stein, via Zoom, for and with John C. Jennings, in court, Personal Representative.

The court advises that it has been waiting for the filing of either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of status of administration and an inventory and appraisal (I&A). Counsel replies that due to a breakdown in communication with his client, he could file a report but that it would be devoid of any information and that he doesn't believe that his client is willing to sign any documents at this time. The court reminds Mr. Jennings that he needs to work with his attorney to sign any documents required by this court or the court may have no other option but to suspend him. Mr. Stein reports that he has not filed a motion to be relieved as counsel in the related San Mateo County matter. The court reminds Mr. Stein and Mr. Jennings that the court needs an I&A and a petition to approve status of administration before the next calling of this matter. Mr. Jennings requests that the court copy him in all emails to his attorney and provides his email address in open court.

After additional discussion, the Status hearing is continued to Nov-30-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Lozada. Not Reported.
